summary: I grew up in Madison, Wisconsin, after my family moved there in the late 1960s. We lived at Truax Field, which is now where the MATC parking lot is. Madison was very different back then; East Towne Mall and West Towne Mall weren't built yet, and it was mostly farmland around. Shopping was mainly downtown at places like Gimbels, Manchester's, and Rennebaum's. I went to elementary and high school there before joining the military.

I was actually born in Japan, at Johnson Air Base in Tokyo, because my dad was stationed there in the Air Force. Although I was only one year old when we left, I always wanted to go back to Japan, and when I joined the military, I was stationed at Misawa Air Base. It was a dream come true to have my first dorm there, and my daughter was even born in Japan in 1998. Japan holds a special place in my life because of my family's military ties.
cues: born in Tokyo, Johnson Air Base

summary: My family was stationed at Truax Field when we moved back to Madison in the late 1960s. We actually lived in a trailer right there, where the MATC parking lot is now. I remember the big buildings and the SAGE building still standing there. It was a military environment and part of my early childhood memories in Madison.

I was born at Johnson Air Base in Tokyo, Japan, while my dad was on active duty in the Air Force. Although I don't remember growing up there since I left when I was one, it was the place of my birth and my dad's station at the time. It connects my family's military history to Japan.

When I was growing up at Truax Field in Madison, I remember the SAGE building clearly. It was one of the big buildings still standing there during that time. It was part of the military environment around where we lived in the late 1960s.

We lived at Truax Field where the MATC parking lot is now, so I remember that area before MATC was built. When I was a kid, MATC wasn't there yet, and the area was mostly farmland. It’s interesting to see how much that place has changed since then.

East Towne Mall wasn't built when I was growing up in Madison in the late 1960s. The area was mostly farmland, so there weren't many shopping options except downtown. It’s amazing how much the city has developed since then.

Like East Towne Mall, West Towne Mall also didn't exist when I was growing up in Madison. The surrounding area was mostly farmland, and shopping was limited to downtown stores. It’s a reminder of how much Madison has grown and changed over the years.

When I was a kid in Madison, the only places to shop were downtown, and Gimbels was one of the main stores we went to with my parents. It was part of my childhood memories growing up in the late 1960s before the malls were built. Shopping downtown was a regular family activity.

Manchester's was another downtown Madison store where my family shopped when I was growing up in the late 1960s. Since malls like East Towne and West Towne weren't built yet, Manchester's was one of the few shopping options. It was part of the community experience in Madison back then.

Rennebaum's was one of the downtown stores in Madison where my family shopped during my childhood in the late 1960s. It was one of the few places to go before the malls were developed. Shopping downtown was a common family outing for us.

My dad was in the United States Air Force, which influenced my life greatly. I followed in his footsteps and enlisted myself, first in the Air National Guard in 1980 and then active duty in 1985. I was proud to be one of the first black females in the 115th Fighter Wing, and my military career took me to places like Misawa Air Base in Japan. The Air Force shaped much of my identity and life experiences.

When I enlisted in the Air National Guard in 1980, I became one of the first black females to join the 115th Fighter Wing. It was a significant milestone for me, and I was proud to be part of that unit. This was before I went active duty in 1985, and it marked the beginning of my military career.

I went through basic training twice at Lackland Air Force Base in Texas. The first time was when I enlisted active duty in 1985, even though I had already been in the Guard. I earned an oak leaf cluster on my graduation ribbon because of that second basic training. Lackland was where I experienced both the fun of drum and bugle corps and the tougher side of basic training the second time around.

After completing basic training, I was stationed at Misawa Air Base in Japan, which was my first duty station on active duty. I was thrilled to be back in Japan, fulfilling a childhood dream. Misawa was where I served as a cook and where my daughter was born in 1998. It was a meaningful place in my military journey.

During my first basic training at Lackland, I was recruited into the drum and bugle corps, which was like the Air Force band but with drum corps instruments. I played the contrabass and was the first female to do so, which caused some friction with the guys. It made basic training more fun and laid back for me, and I got to perform at graduations and parades. It was a unique and proud part of my military experience.

While I was in the Air Guard in the early 1980s, I made a friend named Lynne LaPlant. We took a trip to Milwaukee and got coffee at a place called Blackhawk. It was a fun memory from my time in the Guard, traveling and bonding with fellow airmen.

During my time in the Air Guard in the early 1980s, we traveled a lot on orders, sometimes to places like California. These trips were often on dummy orders when planes had extra seats, so we got to fly along and see different places. It was part of the exciting and varied experiences I had while serving.

I had the chance to travel to Hawaii during my time in the Air Guard in the early 1980s. We often went on orders when planes had available seats, which allowed us to visit places like Hawaii. While there, I played on the softball team and enjoyed the experience. It was a memorable part of my military life.

Florida was one of the destinations I traveled to while serving in the Air Guard in the early 1980s. Like other trips to California and Hawaii, these were often on dummy orders when planes had extra seats. Traveling to Florida was part of the fun and diverse experiences I had during my military service.
